sourceText:
Source title:
How to Brew the Perfect Chemex

Source summary:
A step-by-step guide to brewing with the Chemex pour-over coffee maker.

Page text:
How to Brew the Perfect Chemex
by Blue Bottle Coffee

The Chemex is one of the most elegant ways to brew coffee. Here's our go-to recipe.

You'll need:
- 42g of coffee, ground to a medium-coarse consistency (like sea salt)
- 700g of water at 205°F
- Chemex with bonded filter

Brewing:
1. Rinse your filter with hot water, discard the rinse water
2. Add grounds to the filter, start your timer
3. Bloom: Pour 100g of water over the grounds in a spiral motion. Wait 45 seconds.
4. Beginning at 0:45, pour slowly in concentric circles to 400g. Take about 1 minute 15 seconds.
5. At about 2:00, pour to 700g over 1 minute.
6. Let the coffee drain completely.

Your total brew time should land between 4:00 and 4:30.

Troubleshooting: If the brew drains too fast, try grinding finer. If too slow, go coarser.

Source URL:
https://bluebottlecoffee.com/brew-guides/chemex
